sourceMovie.title: Ghosts
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ghosts (1997) stuck because it commits to its music premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Stan Winston's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The music and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Descendants 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghosts clicked for you, Descendants 3 (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in music, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ghosts?
faq.answer: Try Descendants 3, Anima, Scooby-Doo! and the Legend of the Vampire—each shares DNA with Ghosts while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ghosts?
faq.answer: Ghosts (1997) is best described as Music, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ghosts worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.0/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this music lane.
